User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the Cyber Gladiators application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: Role of the user (Teacher or Student).

xp.description: Total experience points earned by the user.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents a quiz created by a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quiz entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the quiz was created.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a question within a quiz.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

correctAnswerIndex.description: Index of the correct answer in the answerOptions array.

timer.description: Timer duration (in seconds) for the question.

BattleRoom.title: BattleRoom
BattleRoom.description: Represents a battle room where students can take quizzes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BattleRoom entity.

roomCode.description: Unique code for students to join the battle room.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the battle room was created.

studentIds.description: References to Users (Students) in the BattleRoom. (Relationship: BattleRoom N:N User)

BattleResult.title: BattleResult
BattleResult.description: Represents the result of a student's participation in a battle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BattleResult entity.

xpEarned.description: XP earned by the student in the battle.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the battle was completed by the student.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership simplifies security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores quizzes created by teachers. Path-based ownership for teacher access.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the teacher.

params.name: quizId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quiz.

definition.description: Stores questions within a quiz. Path-based ownership inheriting teacher and quiz context.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the teacher.

params.name: quizId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quiz.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

definition.description: Stores battle room information.  Includes studentIds array for access control.

params.name: battleRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier for the battle room.

definition.description: Stores battle results. Contains battleRoomId and studentId for querying and access control.

params.name: battleResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for the battle result.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the Cyber Gladiators application, focusing on role-based access control, quiz creation, battle room management, and result tracking. The design emphasizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability.   It utilizes path-based ownership for user data and quizzes and a membership map to manage access to battle rooms.  Denormalization is crucial for authorization independence, copying necessary attributes to child documents, avoiding the need for `get()` calls in security rules, and enabling atomic operations.  Each collection will contain documents that share the same security requirements to adhere to the Homogeneous Security Posture design principle. 

*   User Data (`/users/{userId}`): Stores user profiles. This is path-based ownership, providing straightforward security rules based on `request.auth.uid`. No denormalization is needed here as access is directly tied to the authenticated user.
*   Quizzes (`/users/{teacherId}/quizzes/{quizId}`): Quizzes are stored as subcollections under the teacher's document. This establishes a clear ownership model, simplifying security rules.  The `teacherId` is implicit in the path, so no denormalization of ownership is required within the quiz document itself.
*   Questions (`/users/{teacherId}/quizzes/{quizId}/questions/{questionId}`): Questions are stored as subcollections of quizzes. This maintains the ownership hierarchy.  Each question inherently inherits the `teacherId` via its path, avoiding the need for denormalization within the question documents.
*   Battle Rooms (`/battleRooms/{battleRoomId}`): Battle rooms are stored in a top-level collection. The `quizId` is stored on the battleRoom document. It stores an array of studentIds that are in the room. Security rules enforce teacher-only write access based on the quiz's `teacherId` which is then copied into the room for simpler access control. Student read/write access is controlled via the `studentIds` array.
*   Battle Results (`/battleResults/{battleResultId}`): Battle results are stored in a top-level collection. Each document contains the `battleRoomId` and `studentId`, enabling efficient queries for battle results by room or student.  Security rules are straightforward as they can directly check `request.auth.uid` against the `studentId`.

This design ensures Authorization Independence by avoiding `get()` calls in rules. For example, accessing a quiz or question doesn't require fetching the teacher's document; the path itself confirms ownership.  For battle rooms, the denormalized `quiz.teacherId` and the `studentIds` array enable secure access control without needing to retrieve parent documents.  This design also supports the required QAPs by clearly separating resources and using well-defined ownership and membership models. All documents within each collection share the same security posture, ensuring that listing operations are secure.
